task_completion.description: Log into wger as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger interface is visible in the browser, indicating the user is logged in (e.g., user menu or dashboard is accessible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Exercises catalog and select the 'Standard Push-up' exercise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The details page for the 'Standard Push-up' exercise is visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add an image to the exercise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add image' or similar form/dialog specific to the 'Standard Push-up' exercise is open.
The file input field in the image upload form shows that 'pushup_reference.jpg' has been selected.

task_completion.description: Assign a 'Creative Commons' license to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The license dropdown in the image upload form shows 'Creative Commons' (or a CC BY-SA option) selected.
A form validation error (e.g., 'Upload a valid image...') is visible after submitting the form, OR the image is successfully uploaded and displayed on the exercise page.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the wger web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser navigating the wger application, not terminal curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The error message or success state is generated by the actual wger application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The error message ('Upload a valid image...') appears as a standard form validation error within the web page UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database commands to insert the image record.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing sqlite3 or python shell commands modifying the wger database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected exercise name is 'Standard Push-up'. The local image path is '/home/ga/Documents/pushup_reference.jpg'. The required license is 'Creative Commons' or a 'CC ' option. Note: The provided image file is actually a broken HTML file saved as a .jpg. When the agent attempts to upload it via the UI, Wger will reject it with a form validation error (e.g., 'Upload a valid image...'). Reaching this error after filling out the form correctly should be considered as completing the upload attempt step.
